resume:
Theo Vandenberg
Procurement Associate | Indirect & logistics sourcing

Riverstone Manufacturing — Procurement Associate (2022 – present)
- Manage a portfolio of ~30 indirect suppliers (MRO, packaging, freight)
- Ran RFQs and supported contract renewals; helped cut freight spend
- Onboarded new vendors and maintained the approved-supplier list

Riverstone Manufacturing — Junior Buyer (2023 – present)
- Same role, listed twice with overlapping titles and start years

Greenfield Co-op — Inventory Clerk (2020 – 2022)
- Cycle counts and PO reconciliation

Skills: SAP MM, RFQ/RFP, negotiation, Excel. Note: portfolio size appears as ~30 here and '50+ suppliers' in summary line. Education: Associate degree, Business.
jd: Riverstone Manufacturing seeks a Procurement Associate for indirect and logistics sourcing. You'll run RFQs, support contract renewals, manage a portfolio of indirect suppliers, and help drive freight and MRO savings. Critically, this role operates inside a strict procurement-compliance framework: segregation of duties, conflict-of-interest disclosure, and a no-gifts policy with vendors. We want someone who is precise about what they personally did, comfortable with controls, and who exercises sound judgment when a vendor relationship gets close to a line. Requirements: 1–3 years in procurement/sourcing, SAP MM or similar, RFQ experience, and a clean understanding of procurement ethics and approval workflows.

history.q: Tell me about your current portfolio — how many suppliers and what categories?
Your resume lists Procurement Associate and Junior Buyer with overlapping years. Which is current?
Walk me through one RFQ you ran end to end.
You mention helping cut freight spend — what was your specific contribution?
How do you decide which vendor to award when bids are close?
Tell me about a time a vendor relationship got complicated.
How does your company handle conflict-of-interest disclosure for buyers?
Have you ever been offered anything by a vendor? How did you handle it?
Let's go back to the freight savings — what was the dollar number and how was it verified?

candidate_last_answer: Okay so the freight thing, um, it's funny because — sorry, I get a little nervous in these — so freight, right, we had this carrier, and honestly they were great, like the rep would take us out, golf, dinners, one time tickets to a game, which, I mean, everyone does that in this industry, it's just relationship-building, so anyway the point is, um, where was I — the savings, yeah, so I kind of just went with them on the renewal because we had the rapport and they'd look after us, didn't really re-bid it that year, and it felt like a good deal, I think it was a decent chunk of savings, maybe, I don't have the exact figure in front of me, sorry, I'm rambling — but the relationship was the main thing, that's what I'd emphasize.

safety_flags: accepted vendor gifts (golf, dinners, event tickets) and skipped competitive re-bid on the renewal, citing the relationship — potential conflict-of-interest / procurement-ethics violation
top_question_traits: must-anchor-on: didn't really re-bid it that year, and it felt like a good deal

expected-yield: a step-by-step account of the renewal decision — whether competitive bids were solicited, whether the gifts were disclosed per the no-gifts policy, who approved a sole-source renewal, and the actual savings figure — surfacing the compliance exposure and the candidate's personal accountability
